intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport time\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\nROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n\n# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                router_ms=router_ms,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n\n            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_first_source)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                topic_type=\"exception\",\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n            topic_id = None\n\n        # Handle terminal failure (updates intent status, stores in bead_watch, broadcasts SSE)\n        await handle_terminal_failure(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=\"stuck\",\n                status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                        \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                        \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                        \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                        \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"stuck\",\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": \"Fenced bead detected during intent routing\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create stuck card from fence: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to create stuck card from fence context\",\n            }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = await store.get_fenced_beads_for_session(\n                routed_intent.session_id\n            )\n\n            # If there are fenced beads, extract fence context and create stuck card\n            if fenced_beads:\n                # Get the most recently fenced bead\n                most_recent_fenced = fenced_beads[0]\n                bead_ref = most_recent_fenced[\"bead_ref\"]\n                refusal_reason = most_recent_fenced.get(\"last_refusal_reason\", \"Fenced (no reason provided)\")\n                refusal_count = most_recent_fenced.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n                fence_context = {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} \"\n                    f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                )\n\n                # Create stuck card with fence context\n                return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = await self._check_fence_for_bead(result.bead_id)\n                if fence_context:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Detected immediate fence for bead {result.bead_id} \"\n                        f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                    )\n                    # Return stuck card instead of pending card\n                    return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to escalate to bead\",\n            }\n\n\n# Global router instance\n_router: Optional[IntentRouter] = None\n\n\ndef get_router(store=None) -> IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global intent router instance.\"\"\"\n    global _router\n    if _router is None:\n        _router = IntentRouter(store=store)\n    return _router\n","numLines":853,"startLine":1,"totalLines":853}}}

Extract context for rule evaluation\n172\t        context = {\n173\t            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n174\t            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n175\t            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n176\t            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n177\t            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n178\t        }\n179\t\n180\t        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n181\t        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n182\t        action = context.get(\"action\")\n183\t        if action in read_only_actions:\n184\t            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n185\t\n186\t        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n187\t        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n188\t        for rule in safe_mutations:\n189\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n190\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n191\t\n192\t            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n193\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n194\t                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n195\t\n196\t        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n197\t        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n198\t        for rule in manual_rules:\n199\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n200\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n201\t\n202\t            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n203\t            if action not in actions:\n204\t                continue\n205\t\n206\t            # Evaluate the condition\n207\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n208\t                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n209\t                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n210\t\n211\t        # Check approval workflow rules\n212\t        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n213\t        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n214\t        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n215\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n216\t                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n217\t\n218\t        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n219\t        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n220\t\n221\t    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n222\t        \"\"\"\n223\t        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n224\t\n225\t        Supports simple conditions like:\n226\t        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n227\t        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n228\t        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n229\t        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n230\t\n231\t        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n232\t\n233\t        Args:\n234\t            condition: The condition string\n235\t            context: Dict of available variables\n236\t\n237\t        Returns:\n238\t            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n239\t        \"\"\"\n240\t        try:\n241\t            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n242\t            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n243\t            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n244\t\n245\t            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n246\t            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n247\t            safe_locals = {\n248\t                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n249\t                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n250\t                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n251\t                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n252\t                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n253\t            }\n254\t\n255\t            # Evaluate condition\n256\t            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n257\t            return bool(result)\n258\t        except Exception as e:\n259\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n260\t            return False\n261\t\n262\t    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n263\t        \"\"\"\n264\t        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n265\t\n266\t        Args:\n267\t            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n268\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n269\t\n270\t        Returns:\n271\t            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n272\t        \"\"\"\n273\t        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n274\t        intent_type_mapping = {\n275\t            \"action\": \"action\",\n276\t            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n277\t            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n278\t            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n279\t        }\n280\t\n281\t        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n282\t        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n283\t\n284\t        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n285\t        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n286\t        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n287\t\n288\t    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n289\t        \"\"\"\n290\t        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n291\t\n292\t        Executes kubectl and git commands that are auto-approved based on\n293\t        exceptions.yaml rules, without creating beads.\n294\t\n295\t        Args:\n296\t            request: The escalate request\n297\t\n298\t        Returns:\n299\t            Execution result dict\n300\t        \"\"\"\n301\t        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n302\t        logger.info(f\"Executing auto-approved action '{action}' for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n303\t\n304\t        try:\n305\t            # Route to appropriate executor based on action type\n306\t            if action == \"kubectl_delete_pod\":\n307\t                return await self._execute_delete_pod(request)\n308\t            elif action.startswith(\"kubectl_\"):\n309\t                return await self._execute_kubectl_command(request)\n310\t            elif action.startswith(\"git_\"):\n311\t                return await self._execute_git_command(request)\n312\t            else:\n313\t                # Unknown action - return placeholder for now\n314\t                logger.warning(f\"Unknown auto-approved action: {action}\")\n315\t                return {\n316\t                    \"status\": \"pending\",\n317\t                    \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved action '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n318\t                    \"data\": {\n319\t                        \"action\": action,\n320\t                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n321\t                    },\n322\t                    \"urgency\": \"low\",\n323\t                }\n324\t\n325\t        except CommandExecutionError as e:\n326\t            logger.error(f\"Command execution failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n327\t            return {\n328\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n329\t                \"summary\": f\"Command execution failed: {str(e)}\",\n330\t                \"data\": {\n331\t                    \"action\": action,\n332\t                    \"error\": str(e),\n333\t                },\n334\t                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n335\t            }\n336\t        except Exception as e:\n337\t            logger.error(f\"Unexpected error executing auto-approved action: {e}\")\n338\t            return {\n339\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n340\t                \"summary\": f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n341\t                \"data\": {\n342\t                    \"action\": action,\n343\t                    \"error\": str(e),\n344\t                },\n345\t                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n346\t            }\n347\t\n348\t    async def _execute_delete_pod(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n349\t        \"\"\"\n350\t        Execute kubectl delete pod command.\n351\t\n352\t        Args:\n353\t            request: The escalate request\n354\t\n355\t        Returns:\n356\t            Execution result dict\n357\t        \"\"\"\n358\t        executor = get_kubectl_executor()\n359\t\n360\t        # TODO: Implement other kubectl commands\n386\t        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n387\t        return {\n388\t            \"status\": \"pending\",\n389\t            \"summary\": f\"Kubectl command '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n390\t            \"data\": {\n391\t                \"action\": action,\n392\t                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n393\t            },\n394\t            \"urgency\": \"low\",\n395\t        }\n396\t\n397\t    async def _execute_git_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n398\t        \"\"\"\n399\t        Execute git commands (placeholder for future).\n400\t\n401\t        Args:\n402\t            request: The escalate request\n403\t\n404\t        Returns:\n405\t            Execution result dict\n406\t        \"\"\"\n407\t        # Build context for the LLM\n432\t        context_parts = [\n433\t            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n434\t            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n435\t        ]\n436\t\n437\t        if request.project_slug:\n438\t            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n439\t\n440\t        if request.topic_id:\n441\t            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n442\t\n443\t        # Add any additional context\n444\t        if request.context:\n445\t            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n446\t            for key, value in request.context.items():\n447\t                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n448\t\n449\t        # Build the user message\n450\t        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n451\t            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n452\t            \"\",\n453\t            *context_parts,\n454\t            \"\",\n455\t            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n456\t        ])\n457\t\n458\t        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n459\t\n460\t        # Make LLM call\n461\t        try:\n462\t            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n463\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n464\t                user_message=user_message,\n465\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n466\t                max_tokens=4096,\n467\t                temperature=0.7,\n468\t            )\n469\t\n470\t            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n471\t            return bead_body\n472\t\n473\t        except Exception as e:\n474\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n475\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n476\t\n477\t    async def create_bead(\n478\t        self,\n479\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n480\t        bead_body: str,\n481\t    ) -> str:\n482\t        \"\"\"\n483\t        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n484\t\n485\t        Returns the bead ID.\n486\t        \"\"\"\n487\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n488\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n489\t\n490\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n491\t        metadata = {\n492\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n493\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n494\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n495\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n496\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n497\t        }\n498\t\n499\t        if request.project_slug:\n500\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n501\t\n502\t        if request.topic_id:\n503\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n504\t\n505\t        # Build bf create command\n506\t        # Using bf CLI to create the bead\n507\t        # Note: bf uses --description for the main body content\n508\t        try:\n509\t            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n510\t            cmd = [\n511\t                \"bf\",\n512\t                \"create\",\n513\t                \"--title\", title,\n514\t                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n515\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n516\t            ]\n517\t\n518\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n519\t            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n520\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n521\t                if value is not None:\n522\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n523\t\n524\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n525\t                *cmd,\n526\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n527\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n528\t            )\n529\t\n530\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n531\t\n532\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n533\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n534\t                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n535\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n536\t\n537\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n538\t            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n539\t            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n540\t\n541\t            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n542\t            return bead_id\n543\t\n544\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n545\t            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n546\t            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n547\t        except Exception as e:\n548\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n549\t            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n550\t\n551\t    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n552\t        self,\n553\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n554\t        bead_body: str,\n555\t        bead_type: str,\n556\t    ) -> str:\n557\t        \"\"\"\n558\t        Create a bead with a specific type using the bf CLI.\n559\t\n560\t        Returns the bead ID.\n561\t        \"\"\"\n562\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n563\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n564\t\n565\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n566\t        metadata = {\n567\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n568\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n569\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n570\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n571\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n572\t        }\n573\t\n574\t        if request.project_slug:\n575\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n576\t\n577\t        if request.topic_id:\n578\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n579\t\n580\t        # Add labels for metadata tracking\n591\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n592\t                if value is not None:\n593\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n594\t\n595\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n596\t                *cmd,\n597\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n598\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n599\t            )\n600\t\n601\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n602\t\n603\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n604\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n605\t                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n606\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n607\t\n608\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n609\t            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n610\t            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n611\t\n612\t            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} (type: {bead_type}) for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n613\t            return bead_id\n614\t\n615\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n616\t            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n617\t            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n618\t        except Exception as e:\n619\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n620\t            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n621\t\n622\t    async def _create_bead_watch(\n623\t        self,\n624\t        bead_ref: str,\n625\t        project_slug: str | None,\n626\t        intent_type: str,\n627\t    ) -> None:\n628\t        \"\"\"Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking.\n629\t\n630\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA.\n631\t        SLA defaults by intent_type, with per-project sla_hours override.\n632\t\n633\t        Args:\n634\t            bead_ref: The bead ID to watch\n635\t            project_slug: Project slug for SLA override lookup\n636\t            intent_type: Intent type for default SLA lookup\n637\t        \"\"\"\n638\t        from ..registry import get_project\n639\t\n640\t        sla_hours = None\n641\t\n642\t        # Check for per-project SLA override\n643\t        if project_slug:\n644\t            project = get_project(project_slug)\n645\t            if project:\n646\t                sla_hours = project.get(\"sla_hours\")\n647\t                if sla_hours is not None:\n648\t                    logger.info(\n649\t                        f\"Using per-project SLA override for {project_slug}: \"\n650\t                        f\"{sla_hours} hours\"\n651\t                    )\n652\t\n653\t        store = await self._get_store()\n654\t        await store.create_bead_watch(\n655\t            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n656\t            sla_hours=sla_hours,\n657\t            intent_type=intent_type,\n658\t        )\n659\t        logger.debug(f\"Created bead_watch row for {bead_ref}\")\n660\t\n661\t    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n662\t        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n663\t        # Use utterance prefix as title\n664\t        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n665\t        if len(utterance) > 60:\n666\t            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n667\t        else:\n668\t            title = utterance\n669\t\n670\t        # Add project prefix if available\n671\t        if request.project_slug:\n672\t            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n673\t\n674\t        return title\n675\t\n676\t    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n677\t        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from bf create output.\n678\t\n679\t        bf create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n680\t        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n681\t        \"\"\"\n682\t        import re\n683\t\n684\t        # Strip whitespace first\n685\t        output = output.strip()\n686\t\n687\t        if not output:\n688\t            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n689\t            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n690\t\n691\t        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n692\t        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n693\t        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n694\t        patterns = [\n695\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n696\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n697\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n698\t            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n699\t        ]\n700\t\n701\t        for pattern in patterns:\n702\t            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n703\t            if matches:\n704\t                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n705\t                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n706\t                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n707\t                return bead_id\n708\t\n709\t        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n710\t        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n711\t        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n712\t            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n713\t            return output\n714\t\n715\t        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n716\t        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n717\t        return output\n718\t\n719\t    def build_pending_card(\n720\t        self,\n721\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n722\t        bead_id: str,\n723\t        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n724\t    ) -> dict:\n725\t        \"\"\"\n726\t        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n727\t\n728\t        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n729\t        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n730\t        \"\"\"\n731\t        return {\n732\t            \"type\": \"pending\",\n733\t            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n734\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n735\t            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n736\t            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n737\t            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n738\t            \"status\": \"pending\",\n739\t            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n740\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n741\t            \"metadata\": {\n742\t                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n743\t                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n744\t                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n745\t            },\n746\t        }\n747\t\n748\t    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n749\t        \"\"\"\n750\t        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n751\t\n752\t        Full flow:\n753\t        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n754\t        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n755\t        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n756\t        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, create bead, return pending-card spec\n757\t\n758\t        Args:\n759\t            request: The escalate request\n760\t\n761\t        Returns:\n762\t            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n763\t            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n764\t        \"\"\"\n765\t        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n766\t\n767\t        try:\n768\t            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n769\t            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n770\t            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n771\t\n772\t            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n773\t            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n774\t\n775\t            if auto_approve:\n776\t                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n777\t\n778\t                # Execute directly without bead creation\n779\t                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n780\t\n781\t                # Update intent in store\n782\t                store = await self._get_store()\n783\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n784\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n785\t                    status=\"resolved\",\n786\t                )\n787\t\n788\t                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n789\t                return EscalateResult(\n790\t                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n791\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n792\t                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n793\t                    status=\"completed\",\n794\t                )\n795\t\n796\t            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n797\t            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n798\t            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n799\t\n800\t            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n801\t            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n802\t\n803\t            # Step 5: Create bead with determined type\n804\t            bead_id = await self._create_bead_with_type(request, bead_body, bead_type)\n805\t\n806\t            # Step 5.5: Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking\n807\t            # (plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA)\n808\t            await self._create_bead_watch(\n809\t                bead_ref=bead_id,\n810\t                project_slug=request.project_slug,\n811\t                intent_type=request.intent_type,\n812\t            )\n813\t\n814\t            # Step 6: Build pending card\n815\t            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n816\t\n817\t            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n818\t            store = await self._get_store()\n819\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n820\t                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n821\t                status=\"dispatched\",\n822\t            )\n823\t\n824\t            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n825\t\n826\t            return EscalateResult(\n827\t                bead_id=bead_id,\n828\t                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n829\t                pending_card=pending_card,\n830\t                status=\"created\",\n831\t            )\n832\t\n833\t        except EscalateError:\n834\t            raise\n835\t        except Exception as e:\n836\t            logger.error(f\"Escalate failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n837\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\") from e\n838\t\n839\t\n840\t# Global escalate handler instance\n841\t_handler: Optional[EscalateHandler] = None\n842\t\n843\t\n844\tdef get_escalate_handler(store=None) -> EscalateHandler:\n845\t    \"\"\"Get or create the global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n846\t    global _handler\n847\t    if _handler is None:\n848\t        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n849\t    return _handler\n850\t\n851\t\n852\tasync def escalate_intent(request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n853\t    \"\"\"\n854\t    Convenience function to escalate an intent.\n855\t\n856\t    Args:\n857\t        request: The escalate request\n858\t\n859\t    Returns:\n860\t        EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n861\t    \"\"\"\n862\t    handler = get_escalate_handler()\n863\t    return await handler.escalate_intent(request)\n864\t\n865\t\n866\tasync def handle_terminal_failure(\n867\t    intent_id: str,\n868\t    session_id: str,\n869\t    topic_id: str | None,\n870\t    failure_reason: str,\n871\t    error_type: str = \"unknown\",\n872\t    bead_ref: str | None = None,\n873\t) -> None:\n874\t    \"\"\"\n875\t    Handle a terminal failure for an intent.\n876\t\n877\t    Plan §10 The
